title: Zoro Stands Against Cabaji
description: On pages 13–16, Zoro faces Cabaji while Buggy’s crew tries to exploit his injuries and turn the duel into a spectacle.
scene: Zoro holds his ground against Cabaji’s tricks, treating the fight as a matter of pride rather than a simple brawl.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: YOUR MATBY'S ODD TALENTS CAUSED US QUITE ABIT OF TROUBLE.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: SO YOU'RE THE BIG SCARY "PIRATE HUNTER." WELL, THIS'LL TEACH YOU TO MESS WITH CAP'N BUGGY'S CREW.

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Zolo's hurt bad! How's he supposed to fight!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: HOW CAN YOU JUST STAND THERE AND WATCH YOUR FRIEND GET KILLED!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hope you enjoyed kicking my wound...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: My goal is to be the world's greatest swords- man...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Is that enough of a handicap for you?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Now I'll show you some real swordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: You want to make a fool out of ME?

summary: The part reinforces Zoro’s discipline and pride, showing that the Straw Hats will not let Buggy’s crew define the terms of the fight.
